googleDrive.author: piotr-agier
googleDrive.authCommand: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action GoogleOAuthFile; powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action RunMcp -ServerName google-drive -Runner npx -Package "@piotr-agier/google-drive-mcp@latest" -McpArgs auth; powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action RefreshGoogleDriveToken
googleDrive.notes: Default day-one Node path for Google Drive because it is easy to run locally with npx and browser OAuth. Treat this as a Google Workspace MCP, not only Drive storage: it can expose Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Calendar tools depending on scopes and enabled Google APIs. It is not Google official and can request broad Drive/Docs/Sheets/Slides/Calendar scopes depending on configuration, so use current-company approved OAuth credentials and prefer read-only scopes where possible. Use provider google-drive-official-remote when the selected MCP client and company policy support Google's official remote MCP custom OAuth route.
googleDrive.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: ask for one intended Drive file or folder name or ID, then return only its identity and basic metadata. Test Docs, Sheets, Slides, or Calendar separately only when that capability is needed.

google-drive-official-remote.author: Google Workspace
google-drive-official-remote.notes: Google's official Drive remote MCP in Developer Preview. Prefer this when the selected MCP client can pass static OAuth client credentials for remote MCP servers and company policy allows it.
google-drive-official-remote.testPrompt: Ask for one intended Drive file or folder name or ID, then return only its identity and basic metadata.
google-drive-official-remote.recommendedUse: First-party Google Drive remote MCP when the selected client supports custom OAuth and company policy requires first-party providers.
fallbackWhen: Company requires first-party Google Workspace MCP
Selected MCP client supports custom OAuth credentials for remote MCP
knownLimitations: May require static OAuth support in the MCP client

googleDrive.recommendedUse: Day-one local Node Google Workspace setup when company-approved OAuth credentials are available.
fallbackWhen: Official remote Google Workspace MCP cannot use custom OAuth credentials in the selected client
The user needs practical browser login on day one
knownLimitations: Scopes can include Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Calendar depending on OAuth grant

gmail.author: gongrzhe
gmail.authCommand: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action GoogleOAuthFile; powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action RunMcp -ServerName gmail -Runner npx -Package "@gongrzhe/server-gmail-autoauth-mcp@latest" -McpArgs auth
gmail.notes: Default day-one Node path for Gmail because it is easy to run locally with npx and browser OAuth. It is a popular community MCP, not Google official, and currently requests Gmail modify and settings scopes during auth, so treat it as stronger than read-only. Use provider gmail-official-remote when the selected MCP client and company policy support Google's official remote MCP custom OAuth route.
gmail.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: return at most five label names and no message content.

gmail-official-remote.author: Google Workspace
gmail-official-remote.notes: Google's official Gmail remote MCP in Developer Preview. Prefer this when the selected MCP client can pass static OAuth client credentials for remote MCP servers and company policy allows it.
gmail-official-remote.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: return at most five label names and no message content.
gmail-official-remote.recommendedUse: First-party Gmail remote MCP when the selected client supports custom OAuth and company policy requires first-party providers.
fallbackWhen: Company requires first-party Gmail MCP
Selected MCP client supports custom OAuth credentials for remote MCP
knownLimitations: May require static OAuth support in the MCP client

gmail.recommendedUse: Day-one local Node Gmail setup when company-approved OAuth credentials are available.
fallbackWhen: Official remote Gmail MCP cannot use custom OAuth credentials in the selected client
The user needs practical browser login on day one
knownLimitations: Default scopes can allow message modification, so keep write actions approval-gated

googleTagManager.author: Stape
googleTagManager.notes: Personal OAuth mode through Stape's hosted remote GTM MCP. The user signs in with their own Google account. No service-account email is required.
googleTagManager.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: ask for the intended GTM account or container name or ID, then return only the matching identity and confirm read access. Do not enumerate all accessible accounts or containers.
googleTagManager.recommendedUse: Fast GTM browser OAuth setup when company policy allows Stape-hosted remote MCP.
fallbackWhen: No company Google Cloud OAuth setup is ready
The user needs quick GTM account/container visibility
knownLimitations: Write/publish-capable workflows must remain explicitly approval-gated

googleAnalytics.author: Google Analytics
googleAnalytics.notes: Official Google Analytics MCP. Use user ADC/browser login with the user's own Google account and approved company OAuth credentials when available. Keep this as the only GA4 MCP provider until the user explicitly requests a new one.
googleAnalytics.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: ask for the intended GA4 property name or ID, then return only that property's identity or one minimal aggregate for an approved short date range.
googleAnalytics.recommendedUse: Official GA4 MCP with Google ADC/browser login.
fallbackWhen: No alternative GA4 provider should be used unless the user explicitly requests it
knownLimitations: Requires Google Cloud CLI or ADC-compatible setup
Requires Analytics Admin/Data APIs and user access to GA4 properties

browserQa.author: Microsoft Playwright
browserQa.notes: Default browser QA MCP. Use for user journeys, consent flows, forms, ecommerce paths, screenshots, and repeatable browser interaction. Prefer this before Chrome DevTools when the task is normal page interaction rather than deep debugging.
browserQa.testPrompt: Open a simple public page and report the page title.
browserQa.recommendedUse: First browser automation MCP for journey QA, consent, ecommerce, forms, and screenshots.
fallbackWhen: Use before Browser Debug for normal page interaction
knownLimitations: Can interact with pages, so sensitive logged-in journeys need user awareness

browserDebug.author: Chrome DevTools
browserDebug.notes: Advanced browser debugging MCP. Use for network requests, console errors, screenshots, performance traces, and DevTools-level investigation. It exposes browser content to the MCP client; tell the user before using it on logged-in or sensitive pages. Usage statistics are disabled by default in this kit.
browserDebug.testPrompt: Open a public page and list console errors or key network requests.
browserDebug.recommendedUse: Advanced browser debugging for console, network, screenshot, and performance traces.
fallbackWhen: Use when Playwright-style interaction is not enough
Use when DevTools-level network/console evidence is needed
knownLimitations: Can expose logged-in page content to the MCP client
Best with Chrome or compatible Chromium browser

bigQuery.author: Google Cloud BigQuery
bigQuery.authCommand: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action GoogleAdcLogin; powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\WebAnalystSetup.ps1 -Action BigQueryAdcBearerToken
bigQuery.notes: Official remote BigQuery MCP. Prefer company-approved Google Cloud IAM with least-privilege roles such as MCP Tool User, BigQuery Job User, and BigQuery Data Viewer. Use read-only datasets, dry-runs or query limits where possible, and ask for project/dataset IDs before analysis. BigQuery does not use API keys. In Codex, if remote OAuth login fails with dynamic client registration not supported, the day-one short-term option is to generate a short-lived ADC bearer token into BIGQUERY_MCP_ACCESS_TOKEN and configure bearer_token_env_var; this usually expires in about one hour and should be regenerated/reloaded when needed. Long-term fallback: Google MCP Toolbox for Databases when the company needs local control, parameterized tools, or allowlisted queries.
bigQuery.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: require a confirmed project and dataset first, then return only matching metadata. Do not enumerate other projects or run a query unless its scope and cost guardrail are approved.
bigQuery.recommendedUse: Official remote BigQuery MCP with company-approved IAM/OAuth; use short-lived ADC bearer token only as a day-one bridge when remote OAuth is blocked.
fallbackWhen: Company approves Google Cloud MCP/IAM access
Need metadata or read-only SQL from BigQuery
knownLimitations: Remote OAuth may be blocked in some clients
Short-lived bearer-token bridge expires and requires reload

clickup.author: ClickUp
clickup.notes: Recommended official remote MCP. Use browser OAuth from the MCP client when prompted.
clickup.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: ask for the intended ClickUp workspace name or ID, then return only the matching workspace identity.
clickup.recommendedUse: Official remote ClickUp MCP when project/task context is needed.
fallbackWhen: User selected ClickUp and OAuth works in the MCP client

trello.author: Jarad DeLorenzo
trello.notes: No clear first-party Trello MCP was found. This remains the current third-party candidate.
trello.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: ask for the intended Trello board name or ID, then return only the matching board identity.
trello.recommendedUse: Third-party Trello MCP candidate only when Trello is selected and company policy allows it.
fallbackWhen: No first-party Trello MCP is available and the user needs board visibility

pianoAnalytics.author: Piano
pianoAnalytics.notes: Official Piano MCP is private beta and requires Piano whitelisting plus an access key and secret key. If beta access is not available, use pianoAnalyticsApi.
pianoAnalytics.testPrompt: Ask get_web_analytics_data for a simple traffic summary.
pianoAnalytics.recommendedUse: Official Piano MCP only when the user/client is whitelisted for the private beta.
fallbackWhen: Piano confirms MCP beta access
knownLimitations: Requires Piano access key and secret key

pianoAnalyticsApi.author: Piano
pianoAnalyticsApi.notes: Fallback path when Piano MCP beta access is not whitelisted. The agent should use direct API planning/scripts instead of MCP tools.
pianoAnalyticsApi.testPrompt: Verify Piano API credentials and prepare a Data Query request plan.
pianoAnalyticsApi.recommendedUse: Direct Piano API connector when Piano MCP beta access is unavailable.
fallbackWhen: Piano MCP private beta is unavailable
The user approves read-only API connector work
knownLimitations: Requires API request planning and credential handling

tagCommander.author: Commanders Act
tagCommander.notes: No clear first-party public MCP was found. Use API credentials until a trusted MCP is supplied.
tagCommander.testPrompt: Verify Tag Commander credentials and prepare a container audit plan.
tagCommander.recommendedUse: Direct Commanders Act API connector until a trusted public MCP is supplied.
fallbackWhen: No trusted public MCP is available
The user approves API-token workflow
knownLimitations: Container write-capable API needs explicit approval

contentsquare.author: Contentsquare
contentsquare.notes: Recommended official Contentsquare MCP. The exact URL is provided inside Contentsquare Analysis setup > Model Context Protocol, and auth is OAuth.
contentsquare.testPrompt: Read-only smoke test: ask for the intended Contentsquare project name or ID, then return only the matching project identity.
contentsquare.recommendedUse: Official Contentsquare MCP when the user provides the MCP URL from Contentsquare Analysis setup.
fallbackWhen: Contentsquare is selected and OAuth URL is available
